title: The Holy Grail War
content:
The Holy Grail War is a ritual battle royale conducted in Fuyuki City, Japan. Seven magi known as Masters each summon a Servant — a Heroic Spirit manifested in physical form — and battle until only one pair remains. The winner is granted the Holy Grail, a device said to be capable of granting any wish.

The ritual was established by three founding families: the Einzberns, the Tohsakas, and the Matous (originally the Makiries). Each family contributed a different element to the ritual. The Einzberns provided the vessel (the Holy Grail itself, which takes the form of a woman — a homunculus). The Tohsakas provided the land — the ley lines of Fuyuki City. The Makiries provided the system for summoning Heroic Spirits as Servants.

The Servant system categorizes Heroic Spirits into seven standard classes: Saber, Archer, Lancer, Rider, Caster, Assassin, and Berserker. Each class has specific parameters and class skills. A Heroic Spirit is typically placed in the class that best matches their legend, though the class can affect their capabilities.

Masters are granted three Command Spells — magical tattoos that appear on their body — which can be used to issue absolute orders to their Servant (once per spell), teleport their Servant to their location, or compel the Servant to use their Noble Phantasm. Command Spells are used sparingly as they are irreplaceable.

The Holy Grail Wars in Fuyuki have occurred on a roughly 60-year cycle. The First through Third Wars produced no winner. The Fourth War (Fate/Zero) ended in disaster when Kiritsugu commanded the corrupted Grail to destroy itself, causing the Fuyuki City fire. The Fifth War (Fate/stay night) takes place ten years later.

The Grail's corruption — a fundamental problem introduced during the Third War — means it cannot actually grant beneficial wishes. Any wish processed through the corrupted Grail is fulfilled through the most harmful means possible. The black mud within the Grail is the concept of 'All the World's Evil' (Angra Mainyu), a conceptual curse capable of warping reality.

title: Fate/Zero — Fourth Holy Grail War Overview
content:
Fate/Zero is a light novel series written by Gen Urobuchi and illustrated by Takashi Takeuchi, published by TYPE-MOON Books. It serves as the prequel to Fate/stay night, depicting the Fourth Holy Grail War that took place 10 years before the events of the visual novel.

The story focuses primarily on Kiritsugu Emiya, the Master of Saber, as he pursues his ideal of ending all conflict through the Holy Grail. The narrative contrasts several Master-Servant pairs and their conflicting ideals of heroism, kingship, and justice.

The seven Master-Servant pairs of the Fourth Holy Grail War:
1. Kiritsugu Emiya & Saber (Artoria Pendragon) — Einzbern representative
2. Tokiomi Tohsaka & Archer (Gilgamesh) — Tohsaka representative
3. Kariya Matou & Berserker (Lancelot) — Matou representative
4. Waver Velvet & Rider (Iskandar/Alexander the Great)
5. Ryuunosuke Uryuu & Caster (Gilles de Rais/Bluebeard)
6. Kayneth El-Melloi Archibald & Lancer (Diarmuid Ua Duibhne)
7. Kirei Kotomine & Assassin (Hassan-i-Sabbah)

Key events include the debates between Saber, Gilgamesh, and Rider about the nature of kingship; Kiritsugu's assassination of Kayneth to acquire Command Spells; the destruction of Caster using Excalibur in a combined effort; and the climactic battle at the Einzbern castle.

The war concludes with Kiritsugu using his last two Command Spells to force Saber to destroy the Holy Grail, releasing the corrupted contents and causing the Fuyuki City fire. This fire is the catastrophe that Shirou Emiya survives as a child.

Fate/Zero was adapted into a 25-episode anime series by ufotable, split across two cours, airing in 2011-2012. The anime is highly praised for its production quality and mature tone.

title: Fate/stay night Routes — Fate, Unlimited Blade Works, Heaven's Feel
content:
Fate/stay night is a visual novel developed by TYPE-MOON, originally released in 2004. The story takes place over approximately two weeks during the Fifth Holy Grail War in Fuyuki City. The protagonist is Shirou Emiya, a high school student with amateur magus training who is drawn into the war.

The visual novel has three primary routes, each with a different focus and heroine:

**Fate Route**
The first and most accessible route. The primary heroine is Saber (Artoria Pendragon). The story focuses on Shirou and Saber's growing bond and her struggle with the ideals of kingship. The antagonist is Kirei Kotomine, who seeks to use the Grail for nihilistic purposes. The route introduces the world and rules of the Holy Grail War. It ends with Artoria being released from her contract to the world.

**Unlimited Blade Works Route**
The second route and primary heroine is Rin Tohsaka. This route focuses on Archer's true identity as a future version of Shirou, who became a Counter Guardian after making a contract with Alaya (the collective will of humanity). The antagonist is Caster (Medea), who uses Rule Breaker to steal Servants, and later Gilgamesh. The route ends with Shirou declaring he will pursue his ideal of being a hero while aware of the costs. Archer allows him to overwrite the future.

**Heaven's Feel Route**
The third route and primary heroine is Sakura Matou. The darkest and most emotionally intense route. Sakura, revealed to be the adopted granddaughter of Zouken Matou, becomes the vessel for the corrupted Holy Grail (True Assassin's master, but actually the lesser Grail). The Shadow — a manifestation of Avenger/Angra Mainyu from within Sakura — begins consuming Servants. Shirou must choose between his ideals and saving Sakura. The True Ending involves Illyasviel sacrificing herself to close the Greater Grail, and Sakura surviving. This route introduced Saber Alter and Dark Sakura as major characters.

The visual novel was adapted into multiple anime versions: a 2006 series by Studio Deen (combining routes loosely), the ufotable film Unlimited Blade Works (2010), the ufotable TV series Unlimited Blade Works (2014-2015), and the ufotable Heaven's Feel trilogy of films (2017-2020).
